label: Adversarial minimax objectives

node.label: Adversarial minimax objectives
node.definition: Competing learners optimize opposing goals, as in generator–discriminator or adversarial robustness games.
node.why_it_matters: The role of Adversarial minimax objectives is to connect Generative modeling, Game theory & decision theory to downstream capabilities including Adversarial examples & adversarial training, Generative adversarial networks.
examples: substrate for Adversarial examples & adversarial training
limitations: A tractable objective is a surrogate, so optimizing it can improve the score while leaving the intended behavior unchanged or worse. Its direct dependencies include Generative modeling, Game theory & decision theory.

dependencies.rationale: Adversarial minimax objectives uses Generative modeling as a reusable ingredient in its defined operation.
Adversarial minimax objectives uses Game theory & decision theory as a reusable ingredient in its defined operation.
Adversarial minimax objectives uses Optimization theory as a reusable ingredient in its defined operation.
